execution_blocker.details: Full v9 multiseed (with MLP features on 773 wells) requires the v9 OOF csv as a baseline. The runner (PID 55706) was OOM-killed at ~340/773 wells under contention from 5+ concurrent ML processes. /tmp/v9_oof.csv never landed. System load avg at decision time = 49-63 (M1 Pro 10-core), available RAM = 0 GB. Re-running multiseed_score.py at full v9 scale needs: (a) v9 OOF complete, (b) ~30+ minutes of low-contention CPU. Per the user's brief, the v8-features fallback path was selected as a directional pilot.

stage1_lgb_multiseed_pilot.feature_set: v8 (no MLP) — fallback per user instruction

delta_avg_minus_perseed_mean.note: 5-seed avg vs mean-of-5 single seeds. NOT vs the best single seed — the best single (seed 999) had well_max=28.34, BELOW the averaged 30.93. Averaging hedges but doesn't beat lucky seeds on tail metrics.

stage2_mlp_multiseed_at_imputer_level.comparison_basis: 5-fold GroupKFold OOF over 765 wells / 3,746,966 rows; MLP+PE-L8 multi-output predicting all 6 formation tops
stage2_mlp_multiseed_at_imputer_level.interpretation_mlp_imputer_only: Multi-seed MLP gives substantial -18 ft on the worst well but 4 of 11 catastrophic-tail wells got WORSE with averaging (+9, +9.5, +5, +15.6 ft). The 11 wells > 60ft remain 11 wells > 60ft. Net overall improvement at the IMPUTER level is -0.49 RMSE / 2.7%; the variance reduction is real but uneven on the tail.

key_findings.stage1_summary: 5-seed LGB ensemble vs single seed: Δ overall RMSE = -0.39, Δ well_max = -0.99 ft (on a 30-well v8 pilot, 3-fold). The relative reduction is ~3% on overall and ~3% on well_max.
key_findings.stage2_summary: 3-seed MLP ensemble at the imputer level: Δ TVT overall = -0.49 RMSE, Δ TVT well_max = -18.05 ft (on the 165-ft worst well). 4/11 catastrophic-tail wells got worse with averaging.

empirical_seed_correlation.interpretation: Very high — varying random_state for both GroupKFold split AND LGB internal seeds together still leaves ~93% of the per-seed variance correlated. This caps the asymptotic variance reduction at ~5% std even with infinite seeds.
empirical_seed_correlation.implication: Multi-seed averaging via random_state alone is a small lever (3% RMSE reduction at 5 seeds, 5% asymptotic). To get more, must break correlation via DIFFERENT LGB hyperparameters per seed, disjoint feature subsets, robust loss variants (huber/quantile), or rank-averaging with the existing XGB+CatBoost members already in v8.

key_findings.expected_at_full_v9_scale: If the rho ~ 0.93 measured on v8 pilot generalizes to v9 features, the full-scale 5-seed effect should be: Δ overall ~ -0.3 to -0.5 (from v9 single-seed baseline ~ 12.0), Δ well_max ~ -1.5 to -5 ft (from baseline 56-166 ft). Modest. Higher gain unlikely without decorrelating moves.
key_findings.private_lb_stability_estimate: Multi-seed reduces well_max by ~3% in pilot — this is small relative to the 100+ ft tail variance. On the private LB shake-up scenario where a single 300-ft RMSE well doubles overall RMSE, 5-seed averaging is unlikely to prevent the worst case. The robust path is: (a) MLP ensemble at imputer level (-18 ft on the worst well, more impactful per dollar) PLUS (b) LGB multi-seed (cheap insurance). Together expect 5-15% private-RMSE catastrophic-tail risk reduction. NOT a 50% reduction.

recommendations.rationale: Pilot data shows 5-seed gives Δ overall=-0.39, Δ well_max=-0.99 on the 30-well v8 subset. With measured rho=0.93, returns diminish past 5 seeds (3% -> 5% asymptotic). 3 seeds (current v8) gets 80% of the benefit at 60% the cost. 5 seeds is the cost-effective sweet spot for the production push given 50-min wallclock budget. >5 is a waste.
recommendations.rationale_mlp: 3-seed MLP ensemble at imputer level gives Δ overall=-0.49, Δ well_max=-18 ft on the 165-ft outlier. Per-fold MLP fit time ~30s (Apple Silicon MPS) — 3 fits per fold in OOF = 75s extra per fold = 6 min total over 5 folds. Cost is small for the catastrophic-tail benefit. Pass averaged MLP outputs as features into the GBM stack.
recommendations.expected_combined_benefit: Stage 1 (LGB 5-seed) + Stage 2 (MLP 3-seed): combined Δ overall ~ -0.5 to -0.8 RMSE, Δ well_max ~ -8 to -20 ft. Compute cost ~+30 min total. Cheapest robustness move on the table.
next_steps: Re-run multiseed_score.py at full v9 scale once the v9 OOF runner can complete uncontested (~30 min feature build + ~50 min 5-seed LGB).

MORE IMPACTFUL THAN MULTI-SEED: explore decorrelated ensemble members — robust losses (regression_l1, huber, quantile), different hyperparameter sets, disjoint feature subsets. The lgb_objectives_runner.py running concurrently is testing exactly this.
Consider median-blend across seeds (instead of mean) for the well_max metric specifically — median is more robust to a single-seed outlier on hard wells.

Stack with XGB & CatBoost (already in v8 cell) using rank-averaging — these are STRUCTURALLY decorrelated from LGB, expected rho ~ 0.7 (vs 0.93 within-LGB-seeds), which would give 10-15% std reduction.

If well_max remains the binding constraint, prioritize the spatial-isolation diagnosis (v9_outlier_diagnostics.json identifies 11 wells with TVT RMSE > 60 ft; many are flagged ISO/MOT/REG). Targeted handling there beats any seed-averaging gain.

expected_runtimes_uncontested.stage2_mlp_3_seed_v9: ~95 min (extra 20 min for 3 MLP fits + 1 LGB run for direct comparison)
